components.name: accordion
components.description: Stacked disclosure panels that expand and collapse on click or keyboard, with optional multi-open mode. Each toggled item dispatches both brut:change (state-machine) and brut:open/brut:close (semantic disclosure) for compatibility with both consumer patterns.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-allow-multi
dataAttributes.description: When present, multiple items can stay open at once; otherwise opening one closes the others

detail.value: boolean (new open state of the toggled item)
detail.open: boolean (new state of the toggled item)

aria: aria-controls (on head, points to body id)
a11y.notes: Heads default to <button type="button"> and receive a sequential auto-generated id (brut-accordion-<n>) when none is set, so panels can reference them via aria-labelledby. Bodies receive a sequential auto-generated id (brut-acc-body-<n>) when none is set, plus role="region" and aria-labelledby pointing to the head id (consumer-supplied attributes are preserved). IDs are assigned via deterministic counters rather than Math.random so SSR-hydrated frameworks (Next.js, Astro, Nuxt, SvelteKit) match server and client output. aria-controls and aria-labelledby are always wired at init whenever a body is present, so screen readers can navigate trigger ↔ panel even when consumers omit ids. Each toggled item dispatches both brut:change (state-machine, with detail.value and detail.open) and brut:open/brut:close (semantic disclosure, with detail.value as the new open boolean) so consumers can subscribe to either pattern. All three events bubble from the toggled item, not the accordion root.

aria.name: aria-pressed
aria.notes: on the rendered .brut-carousel__pause button — toggles between false (autoplay running) and true (paused).

a11y.notes: Autoplay respects prefers-reduced-motion and pauses on hover, focus, and tab visibility change. Arrow keys are RTL-aware. Renders a visible .brut-carousel__pause button satisfying WCAG 2.2.2 when data-autoplay is set and user has no prefers-reduced-motion preference.

components.name: combobox
components.description: Searchable single-select with a text input that filters a static option list and a hidden input mirror for form submission.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the auto-injected hidden input when one is not supplied in markup

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-debounce
dataAttributes.description: Optional integer milliseconds. When present and positive, the input handler is debounced by this many ms. Absent or 0 = immediate (default).

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Value written to the hidden input when this option is picked; falls back to option text content

events.name: brut:change

detail.value: string (selected option value, or "" when cleared)
detail.label: string (selected option label, or "" when cleared)

events.notes: Fires on selection, on text-field clear, and on blur when the current text matches no option label. When data-brut-debounce="<ms>" is set on the wrapper, the typing/filter path is debounced by that many ms; selection (option click/Enter) and blur-clear stay immediate.

formState.name: Hidden input is created automatically when data-brut-name is set; otherwise consumer supplies <input type="hidden">

a11y.role: combobox (on the text input); listbox (on the list); option (on each item)
a11y.notes: The dropdown closes on Escape, outside-click, or any ancestor scroll. Filter is a case-insensitive substring match on option text. The .brut-combobox__empty element is shown when no options match. Clearing the visible text field clears the hidden input and dispatches brut:change with empty value/label, so the form never submits a stale selection. Blurring the input while the text does not match any option label clears the hidden input the same way (simple "clear" semantics — the last valid selection is not restored).

components.name: counter
components.description: Live character counter that mirrors the length of a paired input or textarea and flags overflow.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-for
dataAttributes.description: Required — id of the element whose value length is counted

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-max
dataAttributes.description: Optional fallback maximum when the target has no maxlength attribute

detail.max: integer (0 when no max is set)
detail.over: boolean (true when value > max and max is set)

a11y.notes: Read-only indicator; not focusable. Pair the target input with an aria-describedby pointing at the counter element if the count should be announced.

components.name: date
components.description: Text field paired with a popover calendar; selecting a day writes ISO YYYY-MM-DD into the visible field and a hidden mirror.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the auto-injected hidden input when one is not supplied in markup

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-min
dataAttributes.values: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD), or empty for unconstrained
dataAttributes.description: Earliest selectable date. Days before this are disabled.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-max
dataAttributes.values: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD), or empty for unconstrained
dataAttributes.description: Latest selectable date. Days after this are disabled.

formState.name: Hidden input is created automatically when data-brut-name is set; otherwise consumer supplies <input type="hidden">

a11y.role: combobox (on the field); grid (on the day grid); gridcell (on each day)
a11y.notes: Week starts on Monday. Outside-month days are rendered but visually muted via --out. Outside-click closes the popover. PageUp/PageDown shift the visible month and clamp the focused day to the new month's last day. At init, any descendant numeric segment inputs (input[type="number"], .brut-date__year, .brut-date__month, .brut-date__day) receive inputmode="numeric" by default — guarded so consumer-set values win — so touch keyboards open in numeric mode.

components.name: dialog
components.description: Modal dialog wired to external open triggers with optional scrim, escape-to-close, and any [data-brut-close] inside.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-scrim
dataAttributes.description: Optional sibling scrim toggled in lockstep with the dialog

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-open
dataAttributes.description: Click target that opens the dialog with the matching id

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: Marks any element inside the dialog as a close trigger

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-dialog-title
dataAttributes.description: Optional override marking the element to wire as aria-labelledby when no h1–h6 is present in .brut-dialog__head

a11y.notes: Toggles the [hidden] attribute rather than display:none, and toggles aria-modal="true" in lockstep with open/close. On init, if the dialog has neither aria-labelledby nor aria-label, the component wires aria-labelledby to the first heading (h1–h6 or [data-brut-dialog-title]) inside .brut-dialog__head, auto-assigning an id when needed. Scrim closes only on direct click (not on bubbling from inner content). Body scroll is locked while the dialog is open via Brut.scrollLock (reference-counted so nested overlays unlock only when the last one closes). On open, focus moves into the dialog (first focusable child, or the dialog itself if none) and Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within it via Brut.focusTrap; on close, focus returns to the element that triggered the open. The dialog id is required — components without an id are skipped.

components.name: drawer
components.description: Side sheet that slides in from any edge, opened by external triggers with optional scrim and escape-to-close.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-side
dataAttributes.description: Edge the drawer slides from; auto-applied as a .brut-drawer--<side> class if missing

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-scrim
dataAttributes.description: Optional sibling scrim toggled in lockstep with the drawer

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-open
dataAttributes.description: Click target that opens the drawer with the matching id

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: Marks any element inside the drawer as a close trigger

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-drawer-title
dataAttributes.description: Optional override marking the element to wire as aria-labelledby when no h1–h6 is present in .brut-drawer__head

a11y.notes: Forces a layout flush before adding --open so the slide transition runs from the closed transform. On init, if the drawer has neither aria-labelledby nor aria-label, the component wires aria-labelledby to the first heading (h1–h6 or [data-brut-drawer-title]) inside .brut-drawer__head, auto-assigning an id when needed. Body scroll is locked while the drawer is open via Brut.scrollLock (reference-counted so nested overlays unlock only when the last one closes). On open, focus moves into the drawer (first focusable child, or the drawer itself if none) and Tab/Shift+Tab cycle within it via Brut.focusTrap; on close, focus returns to the element that triggered the open. The drawer id is required — components without an id are skipped.

components.name: field
components.description: Form-field wrapper that mirrors .brut-field--invalid / .brut-field--valid modifier classes onto the child input as aria-invalid so screen readers announce validation state.

a11y.notes: A global MutationObserver watches every .brut-field wrapper for class changes and sets aria-invalid="true" on the first descendant input/select/textarea when --invalid is present. The data-brut="field" hook is optional — the wrapper class alone triggers the observer.

components.name: file
components.description: File picker that wraps a hidden <input type="file"> with a visible button and live filename label.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-accept-label
dataAttributes.description: Optional human-readable hint describing accepted file types or constraints (e.g. "PNG or JPG, up to 5MB"). Rendered into a visually-hidden description element and wired to the input via aria-describedby. Falls back to a generic "Accepted: <accept>" derived from the native accept attribute when omitted.

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input type="file">

aria: aria-describedby (on input, points at the hidden accept-label hint)
a11y.notes: Uses a real <label> wrapping the native file input, so click, focus, and keyboard activation are inherited from the browser. The visible button is purely decorative. On init, the component appends a visually-hidden aria-live="polite" region that announces the selected filename(s) after each change. If data-brut-accept-label is set on the wrapper (or the input has an accept attribute), a visually-hidden description is appended and referenced via aria-describedby so screen reader users can discover the file-type constraint before opening the picker.

components.name: menu

modifiers.name: .brut-menu__item--danger
modifiers.description: Destructive action style for an item (e.g. Delete, Sign out)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-menu-open
dataAttributes.description: Placed on a trigger element; clicking it toggles the matching menu

aria: role="separator" (auto-set on each <hr> inside the menu)
aria-disabled="true" (auto-set on items with the disabled attribute)
a11y.notes: Trigger gets type="button" and aria-haspopup automatically. Items receive role="menuitem" and <hr> separators receive role="separator" during init (existing role attributes such as menuitemcheckbox/menuitemradio are preserved). Escape closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger. Clicking any .brut-menu__item closes the menu. Position recomputes on resize and scroll.

components.name: mono
components.description: Inline monospace run for code-flavored text inside body copy — registered as a static component but the CSS class block is not yet implemented in src/components.css.
components.notes: Component is declared in src/config/define.js with kind:"static" but has no .brut-mono selector in src/components.css. The font-family token (--font-mono) is used by .brut-code, .brut-pre, and .brut-kbd; for inline code styling today prefer .brut-code. preview/type-mono.html is a typography demo, not a usage page for .brut-mono. Treat this as outstanding component-debt.

examples.title: Monospace run inside body copy (intended pattern)
examples.html: Edit the file at src/config/define.js to register a new component.

components.name: multiselect
components.description: Combobox-style field that holds chips for selected values with type-to-filter, keyboard toggle, and Backspace removal.

modifiers.name: .brut-multiselect--open
modifiers.description: Applied while the option list is visible

modifiers.name: .brut-multiselect__opt--selected
modifiers.description: Marks an option as currently chosen (toggled internally)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute used on the generated hidden inputs (one per selected value)

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: On each .brut-multiselect__opt, the value submitted when selected

dataAttributes.name: data-selected
dataAttributes.description: On a .brut-multiselect__opt, marks it as pre-selected on init

formState.name: one <input type="hidden" name="<data-brut-name>"> per selected value, marked with data-brut-mirror="1"

keyboard: Backspace (remove last chip when input empty)
a11y.notes: Click outside closes the list. Filtering hides non-matching options and shows the .brut-multiselect__empty fallback when no options match.

components.name: notice
components.description: Banner-style call-out for advisory or contextual messaging — registered as a static component but the CSS class block is not yet implemented in src/components.css; functionally similar to .brut-alert.
components.notes: Component is declared in src/config/define.js with kind:"static" but has no .brut-notice selector in src/components.css and no preview/docs surface. Use .brut-alert (with --info, --warn, --ok, --err) for any banner-style messaging today. Treat .brut-notice as outstanding component-debt.

components.name: otp
components.description: One-time-code input rendered as one cell per digit with auto-advance, paste-fill, and Backspace/Arrow navigation.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-len
dataAttributes.description: Number of cells to auto-generate when none are present

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute on the hidden input that mirrors the joined value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-label-cell
dataAttributes.description: i18n override for the per-cell aria-label noun (e.g. "Ziffer" → "Ziffer 1 of 6")

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-autocomplete
dataAttributes.description: Override the per-cell autocomplete value; "off" suppresses the attribute entirely

detail.value: string (full code, fires when all cells are filled)

formState.name: auto-created <input type="hidden" name="<data-brut-name>"> with the joined value

a11y.notes: At init, every cell receives inputmode="numeric" and autocomplete="one-time-code" by default (guarded so consumer-set values win); set data-brut-autocomplete on the wrapper to override the autocomplete value, or "off" to suppress it. Together these surface the numeric keypad and the iOS SMS one-time-code autofill shortcut. Non-digit keystrokes are stripped. Pasting a longer string fills cells left-to-right from the focused cell. Each cell gets aria-label="Digit N of M" (override the noun via data-brut-label-cell). A visually-hidden aria-live="polite" region announces "Code complete" when all cells are filled, in sync with the brut:complete dispatch.

examples.title: 6-digit, auto-generated cells
4-digit PIN
Pre-populated cells

responsive.notes: Cell row keeps width across tiers within its container.

components.name: overline
components.description: Eyebrow label rendered above a heading — sans font, extra-small, bold, uppercase, widest tracking, muted concrete-400 color, zero margin.
useCases: category label above a hero headline
components.notes: Differs from .brut-h6 — .brut-overline is muted (concrete-400) and used as a kicker above a larger heading; .brut-h6 is the smallest in-flow heading proper. Pair an overline directly above an .brut-h1 or .brut-display-* for the classic eyebrow lockup.

components.name: pagination
components.description: Page navigation with prev/next, numbered buttons, first/last anchors, and ellipsis gaps for large page counts.

dataAttributes.name: data-total
dataAttributes.description: Total number of items in the dataset

dataAttributes.name: data-page-size
dataAttributes.description: Number of items shown per page

dataAttributes.name: data-page
dataAttributes.description: Current 1-based page; clamped to total page count

dataAttributes.name: data-sibling-count
dataAttributes.description: Pages shown on each side of the current page before collapsing into an ellipsis

dataAttributes.name: data-page-sizes
dataAttributes.description: When present, renders a `.brut-select` page-size picker. The current page-size is auto-included if missing from the list. Omit the attribute to keep existing markup unchanged.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-label-page-size
dataAttributes.description: Label text for the page-size selector; provided for i18n.

events.name: brut:change
events.notes: Fires on page-button clicks and on page-size selector changes. When the page size changes, `page` resets to 1 and `pageSize` carries the new value; the detail shape is unchanged.

a11y.notes: Root gets role="navigation" and aria-label="Pagination" if absent. Active page button carries aria-current="page". Prev/next buttons are disabled at the edges. Ellipsis gaps are aria-hidden so screen readers skip them.

examples.title: Default — 10 pages of 20
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="200" data-page-size="20" data-page="1"></nav>

examples.title: Mid-range with siblings collapsed
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="500" data-page-size="25" data-page="10" data-sibling-count="1"></nav>

examples.title: Wider sibling window
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="1000" data-page-size="50" data-page="7" data-sibling-count="2"></nav>

responsive.notes: Middle pages collapse to an ellipsis on phones; full row visible at sm and above.

components.name: password
components.description: Password input with a SHOW/HIDE toggle button that flips the input type between password and text.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-autocomplete
dataAttributes.description: Override the autocomplete value applied at init; "off" suppresses the attribute entirely

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input> (type flips between "password" and "text")

a11y.notes: At init the wrapped input receives autocomplete="current-password" by default — guarded so consumer-set values win, and suppressed when data-brut-autocomplete="off" is on the wrapper — so password managers recognise the field. Toggle button is forced to type="button" so it never submits the surrounding form. Label text and aria attributes update in sync with the visibility state.

examples.title: Default (hidden)
With initial value

responsive.notes: Form input; no viewport flip.

components.name: popover
components.description: Trigger-anchored card with optional head bar, dismissed by click-outside, Escape, or any [data-brut-close] inside.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-popover-open
dataAttributes.description: On a trigger element; clicking it toggles the matching popover

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: On any element inside the popover; clicking it closes the popover

a11y.notes: Trigger gets type="button" automatically and is wired with aria-haspopup="dialog", aria-expanded, and aria-controls pointing at the popover id (auto-generated if missing). The popover element gets role="dialog" unless the consumer set a different role. Click outside or pressing Escape closes the popover. Position recomputes on window resize and capture-phase scroll so it stays anchored to the trigger. Position auto-flips to the opposite side when the preferred side would clip at the viewport edge.

examples.html: Filters

examples.title: Plain body, no head

components.description: Block-level preformatted code panel — monospace, ink background with paper text, sm size, thick ink border, sm shadow, scroll-on-overflow.
components.notes: Token-color hooks are available for inline syntax highlight: .tok-comment (concrete-300), .tok-keyword (pop-pink), .tok-string (pop-lime), .tok-num (primary). Wrap highlighted runs in <span class="tok-*"> inside the <pre>.

components.name: progress
components.description: Determinate or indeterminate horizontal progress bar driven by a CSS --progress variable, with a JS API for live updates.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value
dataAttributes.description: Initial progress value; mirrored to aria-valuenow and the --progress CSS variable

aria: aria-valuenow (kept in sync with current value)
a11y.notes: role and aria-valuemin/max are set on init. Use el.brutProgress.setValue(n) and el.brutProgress.getValue() for programmatic updates. The optional .brut-progress__label text is updated to the rounded percentage on every setValue.

components.name: prose
components.description: Long-form readable text container — sans, base size, normal line-height, 70ch measure — auto-spaces direct children with a sp-3h gap and tightens spacing around nested headings, lists, and inline code.
components.notes: Owl-style spacing (".brut-prose > * + *") only targets DIRECT children, so put each paragraph, heading, and list directly inside the container. Nested h2/h3 get extra top margin (sp-8 / sp-6) for clear section breaks. Inline <code> inside .brut-prose picks up the .brut-code styling automatically.

components.notes: Use .brut-pull-quote (separate class) for the larger display-weight pull-out with thick top + bottom rules. .brut-quote stays inline-friendly inside body copy.

components.name: radio
components.description: Visual radio control synced to an inner hidden <input type="radio">, grouped by name, with Space/Enter activation.

modifiers.name: .brut-radio--on
modifiers.description: Checked visual state (toggled in sync with the underlying input)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Optional group name when there is no inner radio input; siblings sharing this name behave as one group

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Value emitted in brut:change when there is no inner radio input

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input type="radio">

a11y.notes: role="radio" and tabindex="0" are set on init. Native <input type="radio"> change events keep all siblings in the same group visually in sync, so labels, click, and form reset all behave correctly.

examples.html:

  <input type="radio" name="size" value="lg" hidden/>
</label>

responsive.notes: Form control; lays out with its parent at any tier.

components.name: range
components.description: Restyled native single-thumb slider — bone-fill track with thick ink border and sp-icon primary-fill thumb. Wraps a real <input type="range"> for native value, keyboard, and form submission.
components.notes: Apply the class directly to the <input type="range"> element. Stepped behavior comes from the standard step="" attribute. For two-thumb min/max selection, use .brut-range-dual (interactive component, separate JS module).

examples.title: Default range slider
examples.html: <input class="brut-range" type="range" min="0" max="100" value="50">

examples.html:
<label class="brut-label" for="volume">Volume</label>
<input id="volume" class="brut-range" type="range" min="0" max="10" step="1" value="7">

components.name: range-dual
components.description: Two-thumb range slider that emits a min/max pair, with pointer drag, click-to-jump on the track, and keyboard nudging.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-min
dataAttributes.description: Lower bound of the slider

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-max
dataAttributes.description: Upper bound of the slider

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-step
dataAttributes.description: Snap increment for thumbs and keyboard

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value-min
dataAttributes.description: Initial position of the lower thumb

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value-max
dataAttributes.description: Initial position of the upper thumb

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name-min
dataAttributes.description: Form name for the auto-created hidden input mirroring the lower value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name-max
dataAttributes.description: Form name for the auto-created hidden input mirroring the upper value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-prefix
dataAttributes.description: Prepended to the announced thumb value (e.g. "$") for screen-reader unit context

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-suffix
dataAttributes.description: Appended to the announced thumb value (e.g. " kg") for screen-reader unit context

formState.name: two hidden inputs created when data-brut-name-min / data-brut-name-max are set

a11y.notes: The lower thumb is clamped at the upper thumb and vice versa via aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax. PageUp/PageDown move by 10× step. Home/End collapse to the opposite thumb or to min/max. aria-valuetext wraps the raw number with data-brut-prefix and data-brut-suffix so screen readers announce unit-aware values (e.g. "$120" instead of "120").

components.name: rating
components.description: Row of 1–N star buttons that records an integer score, with hover preview, click-to-toggle, and full keyboard control.

modifiers.name: .brut-rating__star--on
modifiers.description: Filled state applied to stars at or below the current value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Form name; when set, a hidden input is auto-created and synced

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value
dataAttributes.description: Initial score; clamped to 0..N where N is the star count

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-label-star
dataAttributes.description: Override for the per-star aria-label noun (e.g. "estrella"); applied as "<n> <value>"

formState.name: hidden input created automatically when data-brut-name is set

a11y.notes: Container is a radiogroup; each star is a radio. Roving tabindex keeps a single tab stop on the active star (or the first star when cleared); arrow keys move focus and selection together with wrapping. Hover and focus on individual stars preview the score; mouseleave/focusout repaint the locked value. Clicking the active star clears the score (toggle to 0).

components.notes: Each li.brut-rows__item contains a brut-rows__lead, brut-rows__main (with __title and __sub), and brut-rows__trail. Use --bordered + --clickable together for tappable settings rows.

modifiers.name: .brut-search--has-value
modifiers.description: Toggled on the wrapper while the input is non-empty; reveals the clear button

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-debounce
dataAttributes.description: Optional integer milliseconds. When present and positive, the input handler is debounced by this many ms. Absent or 0 = immediate (default).

events.name: brut:change
events.notes: Fires on every keystroke and on clear-button click. When data-brut-debounce="<ms>" is set on the wrapper, the keystroke path is debounced by that many ms; the clear-button path stays immediate.

formState.name: the inner <input type="search"> is the canonical form value

a11y.notes: Clearing the input refocuses it so typing can continue uninterrupted.

components.notes: Sections are full-width by default. Nest a .brut-container inside to constrain content width. Combine background modifiers with --tight or --loose for spacing control.

components.name: segmented
components.description: Exclusive choice button group that behaves like a horizontal radio set, with roving tabindex and arrow-key navigation.

modifiers.name: .brut-segmented__btn--on
modifiers.description: Active state applied to the currently selected segment button

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Form name; when set, a hidden input is auto-created and synced to the active segment

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Per-button value; falls back to the button's text content when omitted

detail.value: string (data-value of the selected segment, or its text content)

formState.name: hidden input created automatically when data-brut-name is set

a11y.role: tablist with role="tab" on each segment
a11y.notes: Arrow keys wrap at both ends. Roving tabindex keeps exactly one segment focusable; activation moves focus to the new segment.

components.name: select
components.description: Restyled native dropdown — bone fill, thick ink border, xs shadow, hard ink chevron drawn with two linear-gradients (sanctioned exception per AGENTS.md). 100% width, focus ring flips background to primary-soft.
components.notes: Width is 100% by default — wrap inside a sized parent or grid cell to constrain. Validation states are driven natively via :user-invalid / :user-valid (and the [aria-invalid="true"] attribute). For a searchable rich select with custom rendering, use .brut-combobox or .brut-multiselect (interactive components).

components.name: sidebar
components.description: Vertical app navigation rail with optional collapsible groups; clicking a button-typed group title toggles its closed state.

modifiers.name: .brut-sidebar__group--closed
modifiers.description: Hides the items beneath a group title and flips the title indicator to "+"

modifiers.name: .brut-sidebar__item--active
modifiers.description: Marks the current page link inside the rail

detail.value: boolean (true when the toggled group is open)
detail.closed: boolean (new closed state of the group)

keyboard: Enter / Space (on a group title button)
a11y.notes: Only group titles authored as <button> become collapsible; <h3> or other elements render the same chrome but stay static. Initial closed state is read from the .brut-sidebar__group--closed class on mount.

components.name: skeleton
components.description: Loading placeholder block with concrete-100 fill, thin ink border, and a hard ink stripe sweep that loops every 800ms (sanctioned loader animation). Pair sizing modifiers to match the content shape being substituted.

modifiers.name: --text
modifiers.description: 1em-tall full-width line for substituting a row of text

modifiers.name: --block
modifiers.description: Fixed-height block (--skeleton-h) for substituting a card hero or image

modifiers.name: --circle
modifiers.description: Square sp-12 box for substituting an avatar

components.notes: The shimmer animation is a sanctioned exception to the 140ms transition cap because it is an animation loop, not a transition. Honors prefers-reduced-motion via the reduce-motion media block in components.css.

components.name: spinner

modifiers.name: --sm

modifiers.name: --md
modifiers.description: sp-8 box with bw-3 border — the default size

modifiers.name: --lg
modifiers.description: sp-12 box with bw-3 border — for page-level loading states

components.notes: The 600ms rotation is a sanctioned exception to the 140ms transition cap because it is an animation loop, not a transition. Honors prefers-reduced-motion via the reduce-motion media block in components.css. For determinate progress, use .brut-progress instead.

components.name: stepper
components.description: Numeric input flanked by minus / plus buttons; reads min, max, and step from the inner <input type="number"> and clamps to the step grid.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-step
dataAttributes.description: Direction hint on each button; falls back to first-button=down, second-button=up when omitted

formState.name: the inner <input type="number"> is the canonical form value (no separate hidden input)

a11y.notes: At init the inner input receives inputmode="numeric" by default ("decimal" when its step contains a fractional part), guarded so a consumer-set inputmode wins; this surfaces a numeric soft keyboard on touch devices. PageUp / PageDown move by 10× step. Programmatic input updates suppress duplicate brut:change emissions while still bubbling native input/change. Out-of-range typed values are clamped and snapped to the step grid relative to min.

examples.title: Default — 0 to 99, step 1
Coarse step (5) over 0–100
Decimal step

responsive.notes: Numeric input with controls; no viewport flip.

components.name: switch
components.description: Boolean toggle backed by a hidden <input type="checkbox"> that posts with the surrounding form; click and Space/Enter both flip state.

modifiers.name: .brut-switch--on
modifiers.description: On state; mirrors the inner checkbox's checked property

formState.name: inner <input type="checkbox" hidden> is the source of truth and posts with the form

a11y.notes: The wrapper is a <label> but its native click-forwards to the inner checkbox is intercepted to prevent double-toggling. Native checkbox change events also fire, so existing form handlers continue to work.

examples.title: Default — off
Default — on
Form-bound with name

responsive.notes: Form control; no viewport flip.

components.name: table
components.description: Data table with click-to-sort headers, header select-all checkbox, and per-row select checkboxes.

modifiers.name: --responsive
modifiers.description: Below the small breakpoint, stack rows as cards (requires data-brut-col-label on each td)

modifiers.name: --sticky-head
modifiers.description: thead remains visible while tbody scrolls

modifiers.name: --sticky-col
modifiers.description: First column remains visible during horizontal scroll

dataAttributes.name: data-sort-key
dataAttributes.description: On a th.brut-table__cell--sortable: identifier for the sortable column

dataAttributes.name: data-sort-value
dataAttributes.description: On a td: explicit value used for sorting (falls back to text content)

dataAttributes.name: data-sort-direction
dataAttributes.description: On a sortable th: written by the JS after each sort to reflect the active direction (every other sortable th gets "none")

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-select-all
dataAttributes.description: On a header element: toggles every [data-brut-row-select] in the table

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-row-select
dataAttributes.description: On a tbody row checkbox: marks the row as selectable by the header toggle

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-col-label
dataAttributes.description: On a td (responsive mode): label prefix shown before the cell value when stacked

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-role
dataAttributes.description: On a child element: opt-in empty-state slot. Auto-shown when tbody has zero visible rows; the table itself is hidden in that state. A MutationObserver on tbody re-syncs after row additions/removals; consumers do not need to call a refresh API.

formState.name: Row checkboxes own their own form state; the table itself does not mirror to a hidden input

a11y.notes: Sortable headers are tabindex=0, activated by Space or Enter. Numeric values sort numerically; otherwise locale-aware case-insensitive string sort. Select-all syncs every row checkbox and dispatches a real change event on the underlying input. Auto empty-state: when a [data-brut-role="empty-state"] child is present, it is automatically revealed (and the table hidden) whenever tbody has no visible rows; a MutationObserver keeps this in sync as rows are added or removed.

components.name: tabs
components.description: One-of-N panel switcher: clicking or arrow-keying a tab reveals the panel whose data-brut-panel matches the tab's data-brut-tab.

modifiers.name: .brut-tab--on
modifiers.description: Active state on the currently selected tab button

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tab
dataAttributes.description: Per-tab key; matched against panels' data-brut-panel

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-panel
dataAttributes.description: Per-panel key; the panel whose key matches the active tab is shown, others receive the hidden attribute

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-panels
dataAttributes.description: Optional root in which to look up panels; defaults to the tablist's parent element

a11y.role: tablist with role="tab" on each trigger
a11y.notes: WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with roving tabindex and arrow-key wrap. Panels are hidden via the native [hidden] attribute, so they are removed from layout and the accessibility tree.

examples.title: Default — three tabs, sibling panels
examples.html:

examples.html:

responsive.notes: Tabs scroll horizontally on phones; full row at sm and above.

components.name: tag
components.description: Removable inline pill for filters, selections, or category labels — with semantic and accent color variants.

modifiers.name: --err
modifiers.description: Error state — red background — light text

modifiers.name: --info
modifiers.description: Informational state — blue background — light text

components.notes: Tags are inline and content-sized. Use semantic modifiers for status pills; accent modifiers (ink/lime/pink/blue) for category or filter chips. Combine with brut-tag-input for chip-entry fields.

examples.title: Filter chip
examples.html: draft

examples.title: Status pill
examples.html: verified

components.name: tag-input
components.description: Chip-entry field. Enter or comma commits a tag, Backspace at empty input removes the last, and each chip has an x button to delete itself.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name attribute for the auto-created hidden input that mirrors comma-joined tag values

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-label
dataAttributes.description: Override the accessible name applied to the text input when no aria-label or aria-labelledby is present (i18n hook)

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Set on each .brut-tag chip to provide the canonical tag value; falls back to chip textContent

formState.name: Auto-creates <input type="hidden"> with comma-joined values; name is data-brut-name or "tags"

aria: aria-live="polite" region appended inside the wrapper announces "Tag X added" / "Tag X removed" on every change
aria-label on the text input — defaults to "Tags", overridable via data-brut-label, skipped if aria-label or aria-labelledby is already set
aria-label="Remove <tagValue>" on each .brut-tag__x close button so screen readers identify which chip the action targets
a11y.notes: Duplicate values are silently rejected. On blur with non-empty input the pending value is committed. Clicking the wrapper outside the field focuses the field. Each add/remove dispatches into the live region in addition to firing brut:change.

examples.html: Press Enter or comma to add.

examples.html: CSS JavaScript UX

examples.title: Many tags with custom name
examples.html: bug feature docs

responsive.notes: Tag chips wrap to new lines via flex-wrap at every tier.

components.name: textarea
components.description: Multi-line text input with the same brutalist border, focus ring, and validation states as brut-input.

modifiers.name: --sm
modifiers.description: Small size — reduced padding and font size

modifiers.name: --lg
modifiers.description: Large size — increased padding and font size

components.notes: Always set a rows attribute or a height for predictable sizing. Validation state modifiers are also driven natively via :user-invalid / :user-valid.

examples.title: Default textarea
examples.html: <textarea class="brut-textarea" rows="4" placeholder="Type a message..."></textarea>

examples.title: Error state
examples.html: <textarea class="brut-textarea brut-textarea--err" rows="4">Required.</textarea>

components.name: theme-switcher
components.description: Radio group that flips the active theme via Brut.theme(); writes [data-theme] on the document root and persists the choice.

modifiers.name: __btn--on
modifiers.description: Applied to the currently selected theme button (paired with aria-checked="true")

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-themes
dataAttributes.description: Theme list to render as buttons; each becomes a radio with that data-value

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.values: string (written by JS on each generated button)
dataAttributes.description: Theme name carried by each rendered button

formState.name: Theme is applied via Brut.theme() and document-root [data-theme]; no form value

a11y.notes:
Empty
is enough — the script generates the buttons. Initial selection mirrors Brut.theme(); arrow keys cycle and immediately apply the focused theme.

Custom theme list

examples.html: BRUT

responsive.notes: Inline control; no viewport flip.

components.name: time
components.description: Text-field combobox with a popover hour/minute stepper; emits HH:MM (24-hour ISO) with optional 12-hour AM/PM display and minute snapping.

modifiers.name: --open
modifiers.description: Applied while the popover is visible

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the hidden input mirror; if absent, no hidden input is auto-created

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-mode
dataAttributes.description: Display mode for the hour stepper; output is always 24-hour HH:MM

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-minute-step
dataAttributes.description: Minute snap interval; values not on the grid are rounded to the nearest step

formState.name: Hidden input inside the wrapper carries HH:MM; auto-created when data-brut-name is set, otherwise an existing <input type="hidden"> is reused

a11y.notes: Outside-click closes the popover. The visible field is readonly; values are committed via the steppers. In 12-hour mode an AM/PM segmented control toggles below the row.

examples.title: 24-hour, populated
12-hour with AM/PM
15-minute snap

responsive.notes: Time picker docks to bottom edge on phones; anchored at sm and above.

components.name: toast
components.description: Transient banner notification with icon slot, semantic color variants, and dismiss affordance — auto-removed by the toast host.

modifiers.name: --leaving
modifiers.description: Exit state — fades to opacity 0; used by the toast host during teardown

components.notes: Toasts mount inside a brut-toast-host container that handles stacking and auto-dismiss timers. The --leaving modifier is applied programmatically and should not appear in static markup.

examples.title: Success toast
examples.html:
✓
Saved. Your changes are live.

components.name: toast-host
components.description: Container that anchors transient .brut-toast notifications spawned by the imperative Brut.toast() API; auto-creates itself if absent.

modifiers.name: --top-right
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the top-right corner of the viewport (default)

modifiers.name: --top-left
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the top-left corner of the viewport

modifiers.name: --bottom-right
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the bottom-right corner of the viewport

modifiers.name: --bottom-left
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the bottom-left corner of the viewport

formState.name: No form value; the host renders ephemeral notifications

a11y.role: status / alert (per toast: "alert" for err and warn, "status" otherwise)
keyboard: click the toast x button to dismiss
a11y.notes: Toasts are spawned imperatively via Brut.toast({ kind, message, timeout, host }) where kind is "ok" | "warn" | "err" | "info". Each toast bubbles brut:close on dismissal. If no host element exists when Brut.toast() is called, one is appended to <body> automatically.

examples.title: Top-right host (default)
Bottom-left host
Programmatic spawn (after host is present)

responsive.notes: Fixed overlay; positioning unchanged across tiers.

components.name: tooltip
components.description: Hover/focus-triggered ink bubble appended to <body> on show and removed on hide; positioned on one of four sides relative to the trigger.

modifiers.name: --left
modifiers.description: Bubble positioned to the left of the trigger

modifiers.name: --right
modifiers.description: Bubble positioned to the right of the trigger

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tip
dataAttributes.description: Required. The tooltip text shown in the bubble

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tip-side
dataAttributes.description: Side of the trigger on which to render the bubble

formState.name: Display-only — no form value

aria: aria-describedby (set on trigger while bubble is visible)
a11y.notes: Trigger can be any element; if it is a <button> the script forces type="button". Bubble is created on show and removed on hide; only one bubble per trigger. Position is computed from getBoundingClientRect with an 8px gap. Position auto-flips to the opposite side when the preferred side would clip at the viewport edge.

examples.title: Default top tooltip on a button
examples.html:
<button class="brut-btn brut-btn--primary" type="button"
        data-brut="tooltip" data-brut-tip="Saved at 12:04">SAVE</button>

examples.title: Four sides
On a focusable input
examples.html:
<input class="brut-input" type="text" placeholder="Email"
       data-brut="tooltip"
       data-brut-tip="We never share email"
       data-brut-tip-side="bottom" />

responsive.notes: Hover-on-pointer; tap-to-pin on coarse pointer / phones.

components.name: topnav
components.description: Sticky page header whose only behavior is the mobile burger toggle for the link list; outside-click and Escape close it.

modifiers.name: --open
modifiers.description: Applied while the mobile link drawer is expanded

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-label-menu
dataAttributes.description: Override for the burger button aria-label

formState.name: Navigation chrome — no form value

a11y.notes: Burger element is .brut-topnav__burger; auto-typed as type="button". Clicks outside the header or pressing Escape close the open menu. Clicking any .brut-topnav__link also closes (mobile UX).

utilities.description: Control the CSS display property of an element.
Set flex container direction and wrap behavior.
Horizontal alignment of flex or grid children along the main axis.
Vertical alignment of flex or grid children along the cross axis.
Override the parent align-items value for a single flex child.
Flex grow, shrink, fill, and order utilities.
Set gap between flex/grid children. Scale: 0=0, 1=4px, 2=8px, 3=12px, 4=16px, 5=24px, 6=32px.
Set margin on all sides or individual edges. Scale: 0=0, 1=4px, 2=8px, 3=12px, 4=16px, 5=24px, 6=32px, 8=48px. Supports auto.
Set padding on all sides or individual edges. Same scale as margin.
Text alignment, transform, wrapping, and truncation.
utilities.notes: Only text-start, text-center, and text-end have responsive variants.

utilities.description: Control font weight.
Override font size using the type scale.
Override line-height.
Override font family.
Width, height, and viewport sizing utilities.
Control element visibility without affecting layout.
utilities.notes: visually-hidden keeps the element in the accessibility tree but hides it visually.

utilities.description: Set CSS position and edge offsets.
Control how content overflows its container.
Text and background color utilities using design tokens.
Add or remove borders using the ink color at bw-3 weight.
Border-radius, shadows, cursor, and interaction utilities.
